name: Kapiolani Park Playground
tagline: Huge banyan-shaded playground steps from Waikiki — the go-to for hotel families who need a playground break.
parking: Free parking along Monsarrat Ave and park side streets; metered spots on Kalakaua
bestTime: Mornings or late afternoon for shade and cooler temps; weekdays for less crowds
talkStory: Kapiolani Park, established in 1877 by King Kalakaua and named for his queen, is the oldest public park in Hawaii. The playground sits beneath massive banyan trees planted in the early 1900s that provide natural shade canopy -- a rarity for Hawaiian playgrounds. The Saturday KCC Farmers Market at the park's edge is one of the best on the island for local produce and prepared foods. Few visitors realize the park also has free public tennis courts and a bandstand that hosts free Sunday concerts.
